creator: leres@freebsd.org
text:
> The Wiki mentioned in the initial PR has been reported to me when
> I asked for help/howto to move away from the Arduino 1.8.6 IDE
> towards a Makefile based environment. I'm new to such kind of
> development, I did my work mostly with make/automake and that on
> a very limited basis.

If you want to develop with make instead of the ide, you might want
to check out devel/arduino-bsd-mk, it's what I use to develop avr
projects.

> To use the Arduino IDE (devel/arduino18) the port devel/extensa-esp32-elf
> is a prerequisite.

Only if you are using esp32; I don't believe the devel/arduino18
port has ever claimed to support architectures other than avr.

Also, you are talking about using a bunch of files that are outside
of the ports system so I don't think devel/xtensa-esp32-elf is at
fault.

>                    Arduino IDE supports ESP32 by cloning/copying
> the arduino-esp32 sources into the sketchbook pathway of a
> user-based development folder and refelct the location in the
> setup of the IDE. So I have
>
> $HOME/projects/arduino.git/sketchbook/hardware/espressif/esp32
>
> containing the necessary sources I need. I'm unaware whether I
> need the ESP-IDF provided by the same repo or even the esptool
> repo, if using Arduino IDE.

As previously stated, the esp-idf tree you use needs to match the
version of the xtensa toolchain. If you only have one toolchain
version available, I would try using a different esp-idf. If you
are unable to change which esp-idf, I would try a different toolchain
version.

I recommend against trying to mix esp-idf and toolchain versions;
in my experience this never works.

> I do not know how the Arduino IDE is determining the IDE necessary,

[assume you meant "the esp-idf necessary"]

> when I compile, I always see that ESP-IDF 4.4-beta is used, no
> matter what ESP32 sources I checked out so far (mostly V4.X via
> tags/v4.x.x, did not try V3.x).

You'll want to figure out how to make the build use a specific
esp-idf.

> To make it short: I would appreciate a more modern xtensa compiler
> (ESP-IDF doesn#t work for freeBSD natively, but the install.sh
> script seems to reel in the 2021r1 version based on gcc8), since
> there is support fpr ESP32-C3 which is suposed to be RISC-V based.
> I have no clue whether LLVM/CLANG is going to support ESP32 and
> its RISC-V sibbling in a sufficient way so one can omit the gcc
> version.

A desirable goal is to be able to build esp32 arduino projects using
only ports. We are currently quite far away from this.

devel/arduino18 is at 1.8.5 (release in October, 2017). arduino/arduino
is at 1.0.6 (released March, 2015) is even older. For starters, one
of them should be updated to 1.18.19 (released December, 2021).

I think all of this is outside of the scope of devel/extensa-esp32-elf.
